acceptance:
## Summary
The repo contract says queue health must stay non-empty and every PR should get an `@codex please review` comment, but the current autonomy tooling mostly summarizes state instead of enforcing those invariants.

## Why This Matters
- queue health is part of the repo's operating model, not optional ceremony
- required review handoff should be automatic if PR creation is automated
- job summaries alone are too ephemeral for real operational continuity

## Evidence
- `AGENTS.md:169`
- `AGENTS.md:170`
- `AGENTS.md:173`
- `.github/workflows/autonomous-shipping.yml:64`
- `.github/workflows/autonomous-shipping.yml:69`
- `.github/workflows/autonomous-shipping.yml:70`
- `docs/autonomy/ACTIVATION.md:95`
- `scripts/autonomy/execute_work_order.py:156`

## Acceptance Criteria
- automation detects and reports zero-PR / zero-ready-issue queue states against repo policy
- PR creation flow adds the required `@codex please review` comment automatically
- queue status is persisted somewhere actionable, not only an ephemeral job summary
- autonomy docs match the implemented behavior

## Notes
This is adjacent to `#96`, but it is better treated as queue-governance tooling rather than generic CI truthfulness.
title: Enforce queue health and Codex review handoff in autonomy tooling
